speaker: FLAVIUS
text: No care, no stop, so senseless of expense

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: That he will neither know how to maintain it

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Nor cease his flow of riot. Takes no account

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: How things go from him nor resumes no care

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Of what is to continue. Never mind

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Was to be so unwise to be so kind.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What shall be done? He will not hear till feel.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I must be round with him, now he comes from hunting.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Fie, fie, fie, fie!

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Good even, Varro. What, you come for money?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: Is ’t not your business too?

speaker: CAPHIS
text: It is. And yours too, Isidore?

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: It is so.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Would we were all discharged!

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: I fear it.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Here comes the lord.

speaker: TIMON
text: So soon as dinner’s done we’ll forth again,

speaker: TIMON
text:
My Alcibiades.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                Caphis
                .
                )
               With me? What is your will?

speaker: CAPHIS
text: My lord, here is a note of certain dues.

speaker: TIMON
text: Dues? Whence are you?

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Of Athens here, my lord.

speaker: TIMON
text: Go to my steward.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Please it your Lordship, he hath put me off

speaker: CAPHIS
text: To the succession of new days this month.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: My master is awaked by great occasion

speaker: CAPHIS
text: To call upon his own and humbly prays you

speaker: CAPHIS
text: That with your other noble parts you’ll suit

speaker: CAPHIS
text: In giving him his right.

speaker: TIMON
text: Mine honest friend,

speaker: TIMON
text: I prithee but repair to me next morning.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Nay, good my lord—

speaker: TIMON
text: Contain thyself, good friend.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: One Varro’s servant,

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: my good lord—

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: From Isidore. He humbly prays your speedy payment.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: If you did know, my lord, my master’s wants—

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: ’Twas due on forfeiture, my lord, six weeks and past.

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: Your steward puts me off, my lord, and I

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: Am sent expressly to your Lordship.

speaker: TIMON
text: Give me breath.—

speaker: TIMON
text: I do beseech you, good my lords, keep on.

speaker: TIMON
text: I’ll wait upon you instantly.

speaker: TIMON
text: Come hither. Pray you,

speaker: TIMON
text: How goes the world that I am thus encountered

speaker: TIMON
text: With clamorous demands of debt, broken bonds,

speaker: TIMON
text: And the detention of long-since-due debts

speaker: TIMON
text: Against my honor?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Please you, gentlemen,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: The time is unagreeable to this business.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Your importunacy cease till after dinner,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: That I may make his Lordship understand

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Wherefore you are not paid.

speaker: TIMON
text: Do so, my friends.—

speaker: TIMON
text: See them well entertained.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Pray, draw near.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Stay, stay, here comes the Fool with Apemantus.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Let’s ha’ some sport with ’em.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: Hang him! He’ll abuse us.

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: A plague upon him, dog!

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: How dost, Fool?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Dost dialogue with thy shadow?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: I speak not to thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text:
No, ’tis to thyself.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Fool
                .
                )
               Come

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: away.

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: There’s the fool hangs

speaker:
ISIDORE’S
               
              MAN
text: on your back already.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: No, thou stand’st single; thou ’rt not on

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: him yet.

speaker: CAPHIS
text: Where’s the fool now?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: He last asked the question. Poor rogues

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: and usurers’ men, bawds between gold and want.

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: What are we, Apemantus?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Asses.

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: Why?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That you ask me what you are, and do not

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: know yourselves.—Speak to ’em, Fool.

speaker: FOOL
text: How do you, gentlemen?

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: Gramercies, good Fool. How does your

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: mistress?

speaker: FOOL
text: She’s e’en setting on water to scald such chickens

speaker: FOOL
text: as you are. Would we could see you at Corinth!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Good. Gramercy.

speaker: FOOL
text: Look you, here comes my master’s page.

speaker: PAGE
text: Why, how now, captain? What do you in

speaker: PAGE
text: this wise company?—How dost thou, Apemantus?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Would I had a rod in my mouth that I

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: might answer thee profitably.

speaker: PAGE
text: Prithee, Apemantus, read me the superscription

speaker: PAGE
text: of these letters. I know not which is which.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Canst not read?

speaker: PAGE
text: No.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: There will little learning die, then, that

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: day thou art hanged. This is to Lord Timon, this to

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Alcibiades. Go. Thou wast born a bastard, and

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: thou ’lt die a bawd.

speaker: PAGE
text: Thou wast whelped a dog, and thou shalt famish

speaker: PAGE
text: a dog’s death. Answer not. I am gone.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: E’en so thou outrunn’st grace.—Fool, I

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: will go with you to Lord Timon’s.

speaker: FOOL
text: Will you leave me there?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: If Timon stay at home.—You three serve

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: three usurers?

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: Ay. Would they served us!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: So would I—as good a trick as ever hangman

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: served thief.

speaker: FOOL
text: Are you three usurers’ men?

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: Ay, fool.

speaker: FOOL
text: I think no usurer but has a fool to his servant.

speaker: FOOL
text: My mistress is one, and I am her Fool. When men

speaker: FOOL
text: come to borrow of your masters, they approach

speaker: FOOL
text: sadly and go away merry, but they enter my master’s

speaker: FOOL
text: house merrily and go away sadly. The reason

speaker: FOOL
text: of this?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: I could render one.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Do it then, that we may account thee a

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: whoremaster and a knave, which notwithstanding,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: thou shalt be no less esteemed.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: What is a whoremaster, fool?

speaker: FOOL
text: A fool in good clothes, and something like thee.

speaker: FOOL
text: ’Tis a spirit; sometime ’t appears like a lord, sometime

speaker: FOOL
text: like a lawyer, sometime like a philosopher,

speaker: FOOL
text: with two stones more than ’s artificial one. He is

speaker: FOOL
text: very often like a knight, and generally in all shapes

speaker: FOOL
text: that man goes up and down in from fourscore to

speaker: FOOL
text: thirteen, this spirit walks in.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              MAN
text: Thou art not altogether a Fool.

speaker: FOOL
text: Nor thou altogether a wise man. As much foolery

speaker: FOOL
text: as I have, so much wit thou lack’st.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That answer might have become Apemantus.

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              MEN
text: Aside, aside! Here comes Lord Timon.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Come with me, fool, come.

speaker: FOOL
text: I do not always follow lover, elder brother, and

speaker: FOOL
text: woman; sometime the philosopher.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Pray you, walk near. I’ll speak with you anon.

speaker: TIMON
text: You make me marvel wherefore ere this time

speaker: TIMON
text: Had you not fully laid my state before me,

speaker: TIMON
text: That I might so have rated my expense

speaker: TIMON
text: As I had leave of means.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: You would not hear me.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: At many leisures I proposed—

speaker: TIMON
text: Go to.

speaker: TIMON
text: Perchance some single vantages you took

speaker: TIMON
text: When my indisposition put you back,

speaker: TIMON
text: And that unaptness made your minister

speaker: TIMON
text: Thus to excuse yourself.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O, my good lord,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: At many times I brought in my accounts,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Laid them before you. You would throw them off

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And say you found them in mine honesty.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: When for some trifling present you have bid me

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Return so much, I have shook my head and wept—

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Yea, ’gainst th’ authority of manners prayed you

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To hold your hand more close. I did endure

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Not seldom nor no slight checks when I have

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Prompted you in the ebb of your estate

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And your great flow of debts. My lovèd lord,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Though you hear now too late, yet now’s a time.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: The greatest of your having lacks a half

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To pay your present debts.

speaker: TIMON
text: Let all my land be sold.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: ’Tis all engaged, some forfeited and gone,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And what remains will hardly stop the mouth

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Of present dues. The future comes apace.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What shall defend the interim? And at length

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: How goes our reck’ning?

speaker: TIMON
text: To Lacedaemon did my land extend.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O my good lord, the world is but a word.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Were it all yours to give it in a breath,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: How quickly were it gone!

speaker: TIMON
text: You tell me true.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: If you suspect my husbandry of falsehood,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Call me before th’ exactest auditors,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And set me on the proof. So the gods bless me,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: When all our offices have been oppressed

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: With riotous feeders, when our vaults have wept

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: With drunken spilth of wine, when every room

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Hath blazed with lights and brayed with minstrelsy,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I have retired me to a wasteful cock

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And set mine eyes at flow.

speaker: TIMON
text: Prithee, no more.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Heavens, have I said, the bounty of this lord!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: How many prodigal bits have slaves and peasants

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: This night englutted. Who is not Timon’s?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What heart, head, sword, force, means, but is Lord Timon’s?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Great Timon, noble, worthy, royal Timon!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Ah, when the means are gone that buy this praise,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: The breath is gone whereof this praise is made.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Feast-won, fast-lost. One cloud of winter showers,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: These flies are couched.

speaker: TIMON
text: Come, sermon me no further.

speaker: TIMON
text: No villainous bounty yet hath passed my heart;

speaker: TIMON
text: Unwisely, not ignobly, have I given.

speaker: TIMON
text: Why dost thou weep? Canst thou the conscience lack

speaker: TIMON
text: To think I shall lack friends? Secure thy heart.

speaker: TIMON
text: If I would broach the vessels of my love

speaker: TIMON
text: And try the argument of hearts by borrowing,

speaker: TIMON
text: Men and men’s fortunes could I frankly use

speaker: TIMON
text: As I can bid thee speak.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Assurance bless your thoughts!

speaker: TIMON
text: And in some sort these wants of mine are crowned,

speaker: TIMON
text: That I account them blessings. For by these

speaker: TIMON
text: Shall I try friends. You shall perceive how you

speaker: TIMON
text: Mistake my fortunes. I am wealthy in my friends.—

speaker: TIMON
text: Within there! Flaminius!—Servilius!

speaker: SERVANTS
text: My lord, my lord.

speaker: TIMON
text: I will dispatch you severally.

speaker: TIMON
text:
You to Lord Lucius, 
                (
                to
                 
                Flaminius
                )
               to Lord

speaker: TIMON
text: Lucullus you—I hunted with his Honor today;

speaker: TIMON
text: you to Sempronius. Commend

speaker: TIMON
text: me to their loves, and I am proud, say, that my

speaker: TIMON
text: occasions have found time to use ’em toward a

speaker: TIMON
text: supply of money. Let the request be fifty talents.

speaker: FLAMINIUS
text: As you have said, my lord.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Lord Lucius and Lucullus? Humh!

speaker: TIMON
text: Go you, sir, to the Senators,

speaker: TIMON
text: Of whom, even to the state’s best health, I have

speaker: TIMON
text: Deserved this hearing. Bid ’em send o’ th’ instant

speaker: TIMON
text: A thousand talents to me.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I have been bold—

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For that I knew it the most general way—

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To them to use your signet and your name,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: But they do shake their heads, and I am here

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: No richer in return.

speaker: TIMON
text: Is ’t true? Can ’t be?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: They answer in a joint and corporate voice

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: That now they are at fall, want treasure, cannot

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Do what they would, are sorry. You are honorable,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: But yet they could have wished—they know not—

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Something hath been amiss—a noble nature

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: May catch a wrench—would all were well—’tis pity.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And so, intending other serious matters,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: After distasteful looks and these hard fractions,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: With certain half-caps and cold-moving nods

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: They froze me into silence.

speaker: TIMON
text: You gods, reward them!

speaker: TIMON
text: Prithee, man, look cheerly. These old fellows

speaker: TIMON
text: Have their ingratitude in them hereditary.

speaker: TIMON
text: Their blood is caked, ’tis cold, it seldom flows;

speaker: TIMON
text: ’Tis lack of kindly warmth they are not kind;

speaker: TIMON
text: And nature, as it grows again toward earth,

speaker: TIMON
text: Is fashioned for the journey, dull and heavy.

speaker: TIMON
text: Go to Ventidius. Prithee, be not sad.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou art true and honest—ingeniously I speak—

speaker: TIMON
text: No blame belongs to thee. Ventidius lately

speaker: TIMON
text: Buried his father, by whose death he’s stepped

speaker: TIMON
text: Into a great estate. When he was poor,

speaker: TIMON
text: Imprisoned, and in scarcity of friends,

speaker: TIMON
text: I cleared him with five talents. Greet him from me.

speaker: TIMON
text: Bid him suppose some good necessity

speaker: TIMON
text: Touches his friend, which craves to be remembered

speaker: TIMON
text: With those five talents. That had, give ’t these fellows

speaker: TIMON
text: To whom ’tis instant due. Ne’er speak or think

speaker: TIMON
text: That Timon’s fortunes ’mong his friends can sink.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I would I could not think it.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: That thought is bounty’s foe;

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Being free itself, it thinks all others so.
